A surprise in Manhattan. It is a interesting building with a interesting story. J.P,Morgan was a avid collector so the museum is small but has interesting books and antiquities.   The entrance fee is like most sites in the city. Unfortunately the guide was a little tedious.

This is for both the library (ticket taker should ensure patrons get a library guide showing the layout, as it’s on multiple levels) and tea.
The tea was lovely.  At $88 for 2 people, it was both a culinary experience and a fun way to share time with a friend.  No reservations taken, but we went early and did not need to wait for a table.

I do wish there were better decaf tea options; a decaf mint tea would be lovely
The Morgan Library once a sleepy space how now become the best New York Museum experience, great curation and always marvelous quirky shows
